channel.title: Gravel on Mizubikes
channel.description: Recent content in Gravel on Mizubikes

item.title: SRAM Force E1 2x vs 1x vs XPLR: Gearing and Gear Jumps Compared
item.description: SRAM Force E1 gearing compared cog by cog — 10-33 vs 10-36 vs 13-speed XPLR 10-46, with the actual gear jumps behind the range numbers.

item.title: Crux 5 vs Road Bike: Is the Quiver Killer Finally Here?
item.description: The Crux 5 weighs 789g, clears 55mm tires, and borrows aero shaping from the Tarmac SL8. Here's the honest case for replacing your road bike with one.

item.title: Specialized Crux 5: The Gravel Race Bike Goes Full Aero
item.description: Specialized officially launches the Crux 5 with aero tube shaping, 55mm tire clearance, and a FACT 12r frame under 790 grams.

item.title: The Crux 5 Is the Gravel Bike We Knew Was Coming
item.description: The Specialized Crux 5 goes full aero and leaves its cyclocross roots behind. Here's why that was inevitable — and what it might cost us down the road.

item.title: 32-Inch Wheels Make More Sense for Gravel Than XC — And Scott Just Proved It
item.description: Scott's 32-inch gravel prototype headed to Unbound makes a compelling case that bigger wheels belong in gravel racing before they ever belong in XC.

item.title: Buying a New or Used Gravel Bike? Check the Dropout Before You Commit to SRAM 1x13
item.description: If you want to run SRAM's 13-speed XPLR groupset, your frame needs a UDH dropout. Here's what to look for on popular gravel bikes — new and used.

item.title: UDH-Compatible Gravel Bikes: Every Frame on SRAM's List (May 2026)
item.description: A full list of UDH-compatible gravel bikes pulled from SRAM's official Bike Finder, updated May 2026.

item.title: ENVE G SES Gravel Wheels: The 4.5 Pro and 6.7 Pro Go Wider Than Ever
item.description: ENVE's new G SES gravel wheels push rim widths to 35mm internal on the flagship 6.7 Pro. Here's what separates the three models and who they're actually for.

item.title: ENVE G SES vs. Zipp 303 XPLR: Aero Gravel Wheels Compared
item.description: ENVE G SES 4.5, 4.5 Pro, and 6.7 Pro vs. Zipp 303 XPLR S and SW — specs, pricing, internal width, and who each wheel is actually for.

item.title: Canyon's New Grail CFR Is the Aeroad of Gravel Bikes
item.description: Canyon's unreleased Grail CFR prototype was spotted at The Traka in Girona this week — and it looks exactly like what you'd expect if you've been watching the Aeroad family tree grow.

item.title: Best Hardtail and Drop Bar Full Suspension Bikes for Gravel in 2026
item.description: From classic hardtail conversions to purpose-built drop bar full suspension bikes, here's what to ride if you want to go beyond what a traditional gravel bike can handle.

item.title: New Specialized Crux: Everything We Know Before Unbound
item.description: A new Specialized Crux is coming — and it looks nothing like its cyclocross roots. Here's everything the rumor mill is saying ahead of an expected Unbound reveal.

item.title: Gravel Bike Tire Clearance: What the Specs Don't Tell You
item.description: Manufacturer tire clearance specs are conservative by design. Here's what real-world clearance actually looks like on the Specialized Crux — and how it compares to the competition.

item.title: Hardtail Gravel Bike Drop Bar Conversion: Complete Parts List
item.description:
Upgrading a hardtail mountain bike into a gravel beast with a drop bar setup is easier than ever with SRAM’s AXS wireless components. Here’s the complete list of parts needed to pull off a clean and functional hardtail gravel bike drop bar conversion. Choosing the right parts ensures your bike remains fast, durable, and comfortable across all kinds of terrain.

Essential Parts Needed for a Hardtail Gravel Bike Drop Bar Conversion

  • SRAM Force eTap AXS Drop Bar Shifters (wireless)
  • SRAM GX Eagle AXS Rear Derailleur
  • SRAM GX Eagle Boost Crankset - 170mm, 12-Speed, 32t
  • Sram XG-1275 GX Eagle 12-Speed Cassette
  • SRAM AXS Batteries and Charger
  • Gravel handlebar: Zipp SL 70 XPLR Drop Handlebar - Carbon 31.8mm 40cm Matte Black
  • Shorter Stem (60-80mm for ideal reach and handling)
  • SRAM Hydraulic Brake Calipers
  • Handlebar Tape
  • Brake Hose/Cable Routing Supplies (if reconfiguring brakes)

Choosing Brake Calipers for a Post Mount Frame

Most mountain bike frames, including hardtails, use post mount brake calipers. This is an important detail when selecting brakes for your drop bar conversion. You have three solid options:

item.title: Hardtail Gravel Bike Conversion: My Cannondale Scalpel HT Gravel Build
item.description:
Gravel bikes are all the rage—but what if you already have a capable hardtail mountain bike frame sitting in your garage? That’s the question I asked before starting a hardtail gravel bike conversion using a Cannondale Scalpel HT.

Spoiler: with the right parts, it might be better than a dedicated gravel frame—especially for mixed terrain, chunkier fire roads, or long-distance bikepacking.

Why Convert a Hardtail to a Gravel Bike?

The Cannondale Scalpel HT is a lightweight, progressive XC race hardtail, and it turns out to be an ideal platform for a hardtail gravel bike conversion. Here’s why:

item.title: 2025 Cannondale SuperX Review: The Ultimate Gravel and Cyclocross Race Bike
item.description:
Why the 2025 Cannondale Super X Is the Ultimate Gravel Race Bike

The 2025 Cannondale Super X is here, and it’s better than ever. With a sleek aerodynamic frame, improved tire clearance, and long-overdue design changes, Cannondale has taken an already legendary cyclocross and gravel race bike and made it even more versatile.

If you’re wondering what makes this bike so special, here’s why the 2025 Super X stands out from the competition.
